mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of a sampling distribution?

options.text: To describe the distribution of a sample statistic
To describe the distribution of the population data
To describe the distribution of a single sample's data
To describe the distribution of errors in data collection

mcqs.explanation: A sampling distribution shows the frequency of different outcomes (like the mean) for a sample statistic across many samples drawn from the same population.

mcqs.question: According to the Central Limit Theorem, as the sample size (n) increases, the sampling distribution of the sample mean will:

options.text: Approach a normal distribution, regardless of the population's shape
Become more skewed if the population is skewed
Always be exactly normal for n > 30
Become less precise as an estimator of the population mean

mcqs.explanation: The Central Limit Theorem states that for a sufficiently large sample size (n), the sampling distribution of the mean will be approximately normally distributed, even if the underlying population distribution is not normal.

mcqs.question: The standard deviation of a sampling distribution is known as the:

options.text: Standard error
Sampling variance
Population deviation
Parameter deviation

mcqs.explanation: The standard error quantifies the variability of a sample statistic (like the mean) from sample to sample. It is the standard deviation of the statistic's sampling distribution.

mcqs.question: If the time a user spends on a webpage is exponentially distributed with a mean of 5 minutes, what is the approximate shape of the sampling distribution of the mean time for samples of size n=40?

options.text: Normal distribution
Exponential distribution
Uniform distribution
Binomial distribution

mcqs.explanation: According to the Central Limit Theorem, the sampling distribution of the mean will be approximately normal for sufficiently large sample sizes (n ≥ 30), regardless of the population distribution.

mcqs.question: Which of the following best describes the relationship between sample size and standard error?

options.text: As sample size increases, standard error decreases
As sample size increases, standard error increases
Standard error is independent of sample size
Standard error equals the population standard deviation

mcqs.explanation: The standard error is calculated as SE = σ / √n, where n is the sample size. Thus, as n increases, SE decreases, leading to more precise estimates.
